description_long: This bird has a medium-sized, elongated body with a distinctive barred black-and-white pattern across its back and wings. Its head features a patch of reddish-orange coloration, particularly prominent on the crown and nape, contrasting with a pale, lightly streaked face and throat. The beak is straight, sturdy, and chisel-shaped, well-adapted for pecking wood. The tail is relatively short and appears slightly rounded with some subtle barring and a hint of reddish coloration near the base. The legs are short and robust, with a muted grayish tone. Overall, the bird's plumage and structure suggest it is well-suited for life in wooded urban parks or tree-lined streets.
description: A medium-sized bird with a barred black-and-white back, reddish-orange crown, and a sturdy chisel-shaped beak. Its tail is short and slightly rounded, and the legs are grayish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bird, highlighting the barred black-and-white plumage, reddish-orange crown, and chisel-shaped beak.
description_view_1: Rear view emphasizing the symmetrical barring on the back and wings, with the tail feathers and a hint of reddish coloration visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the barred pattern, reddish crown, and the straight beak, with the legs partially visible.
description_view_3: Frontal rear view focusing on the reddish-orange nape and crown, the rounded tail, and the overall symmetry of the plumage pattern.
attribute_plumage_pattern: black-and-white horizontal barring on back and wings, reddish-orange crown and nape, pale face and underparts
